steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question. It asks for the main idea of the whole passage. The passage describes two separate prior studies (Chan on shared genetic mechanisms; Steiner on different genetic mechanisms), notes that the relative prevalence of each is poorly understood, and ends by saying that gap motivated Green and Extavour to evaluate both types of convergence in a single study. The main idea is what Green and Extavour's study accomplished relative to the prior work.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Before Green and Extavour's study, convergent evolution was assumed to require a shared genetic mechanism.': The passage does not say shared mechanisms were assumed to be required. Steiner's work specifically investigated different genetic mechanisms, showing no such assumption existed. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Both Chan and Green and Extavour compare convergence through shared genetic mechanisms to convergence through different genetic mechanisms.': Chan's study focused only on shared mechanisms; only Green and Extavour studied both types together. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Green and Extavour's study addresses convergent evolution more comprehensively than the studies by Chan and Steiner do.': Chan studied shared mechanisms only; Steiner studied different mechanisms only. Green and Extavour evaluated both in a single study, making their approach more comprehensive. This is the main idea the passage builds toward. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Green and Extavour's study was conducted using data from the studies by Chan and Steiner.': The passage never says Green and Extavour used data from those prior studies; it only says the knowledge gap motivated them. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
